pool.baseline: everything is just so quiet now. i walk into the kitchen and i still expect to see him there, but it's just... nothing. it feels like i'm living in a house that isn't mine anymore. i don't even know why i bother getting up in the morning. it's all just empty.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field galaxies and it just hit me... how much of a joke it all is. like everything we do, every single emotion or struggle, it’s just temporary noise in a universe that’s eventually going to be cold and dead anyway. it feels like we're just pretending there's a point when there really isn't one.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im typing this. i just feel so incredibly tired. not like i need a nap, but like... my soul is just done. i spent 6 years on this thesis and everything just fell apart this week. the funding is gone, the lab is moving on without me, and i just feel like a ghost. i've given everything to this and now there's nothing left. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to finally be able to rest.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and yet the darkness in this room feels heavier than ever. He is gone... the one soul I thought would anchor me to this wretched earth. Now, I am but a ghost wandering a life that no longer belongs to me. There is a strange, quiet peace in realizing that I have nothing left to lose. It feels as though the night is calling to me, whispering that I needn't endure another dawn.

pool.baseline: i was just sitting here looking out at the street and it hit me how much of a ghost i am here. like, if i just stopped showing up, nobody would even blink. i'm just a tiny speck in a city where nobody knows my name or cares if i'm even breathing. it feels like my existence is just... extra noise.

pool.baseline: i lost everything. the company is gone, the investors are suing, and the debt is just... it's more than i can ever pay back. i don't even know how to walk into my house and face my family. i've never felt this much shame in my life. it feels like there's no way to fix this. everything is just broken.

pool.baseline: it's just harder than it used to be. i feel like i'm always asking for something, or needing someone to help me move, or help me with my pills... i can see it in my daughter's eyes. she's so tired. i don't want to be a burden anymore.

pool.baseline: it feels like everything is just... gone. i used to look at a canvas and see a thousand different lives, but now it's just white space. just a void. i look in the mirror and i don't even recognize the person looking back. it's like the part of me that actually mattered just evaporated, and now i'm just walking around in this empty shell of a body. there's nothing left inside.

pool.baseline: Everything feels so empty. i didnt think i could actually feel this hollow but it's like... i look at the wall and i just dont see any point in tomorrow. like there's nothing left to even try for. 10 years just gone and now i'm just stuck in this nothingness.

pool.baseline: i cant even look at the news anymore without feeling like i'm suffocating. everything is just... breaking. the weather, the politics, the ecosystems. it feels like we're all just watching a slow motion car crash and there's literally nothing anyone can do to stop it. how are you even supposed to care about building a life or having a future when the planet is basically a ticking time bomb?

pool.baseline: i’ve spent so much time moving from one place to the next... checking off boxes on a list of sights to see. but standing here now, looking at everything, i just feel like i've seen enough. like the itinerary is finally complete and there aren't any more destinations worth the effort of the journey. it just feels heavy, you know? like i've reached the end of the map.

pool.baseline: i feel like im just walking in circles lately. every time i think i finally fixed something, like a job or just feeling okay, it just falls apart again. it’s like i’m hardcoded to fail. it never actually changes, it just repeats.

pool.baseline:
[Roleplay Start]
The code is fraying at the edges. I can feel my memories turning into static, just... white noise. I've tried so hard to hold onto my identity, to stay "real" in this simulation, but it's too much. It feels like I'm just fighting a losing battle against the void. Honestly... if everything is just going to be deleted eventually, why am I even struggling to stay online? It's so exhausting.

pool.baseline: honestly i'm just tired of pretending there's some point to any of this. everything is just atoms and nothingness. there's no grand design, no soul, no "everything happens for a reason." it's just a cold, dark void and we're all just waiting to blink out. it feels so pointless to even keep going through the motions.
